title: SUB GEOGRAPHY TOPIC 1 SOLAR SYSTEM 2 LATITUDE AND LONGITUD

questions.question: 1. What is the actual shape of the Earth? / पृथ्वी का वास्तविक आकार क्या है?
questions.explanation: Earth is a Geoid, meaning it is bulged at the equator and flattened at the poles.

questions.question: 2. What is the approximate average radius of the Earth? / पृथ्वी की अनुमानित औसत त्रिज्या क्या है?
questions.explanation: The average radius is 6371 km, with the equatorial radius being slightly larger.

questions.question: 3. Day and night occur due to which motion of the Earth? / दिन और रात की घटना पृथ्वी की किस गति के कारण होती है?
questions.explanation: Day and night are caused by Earth's rotation on its axis from West to East.

questions.question: 4. How much time does Earth take for one rotation? / पृथ्वी को एक घूर्णन में कितना समय लगता है?
options: 23 hr 56 min 4 sec / 23 घंटे 56 मिनट 4 सेकंड
24 hr 56 min 4 sec / 24 घंटे 56 मिनट 4 सेकंड
24 hr 00 min 0 sec / 24 घंटे 00 मिनट 0 सेकंड
23 hr 30 min 0 sec / 23 घंटे 30 मिनट 0 सेकंड
questions.explanation: Earth completes one rotation in exactly 23 hours, 56 minutes, and 4 seconds.

questions.question: 5. What is the total number of Latitudes? / अक्षांशों की कुल संख्या कितनी है?
questions.explanation: There are 90 latitudes each in the North and South, plus the Equator (90+90+1=181).

questions.question: 6. What is the distance between two consecutive latitudes? / दो क्रमागत अक्षांशों के बीच की दूरी कितनी होती है?
questions.explanation: The distance between each degree of latitude is approximately 111 km.

questions.question: 7. Which latitude is known as the Great Circle? / किस अक्षांश को महान वृत्त के रूप में जाना जाता है?
questions.explanation: The Equator (0°) is the largest latitude and is called the Great Circle.

questions.question: 8. Through how many Indian states does the Tropic of Cancer pass? / कर्क रेखा भारत के कितने राज्यों से होकर गुजरती है?
questions.explanation: The Tropic of Cancer (23.5° N) passes through 8 Indian states.

questions.question: 9. Which Indian river crosses the Tropic of Cancer twice? / कौन सी भारतीय नदी कर्क रेखा को दो बार पार करती है?
questions.explanation: The Mahi River is the only river in India that cuts the Tropic of Cancer twice.

questions.question: 10. What is the total number of Longitudes? / देशांतरों की कुल संख्या कितनी है?
questions.explanation: There are 360 longitude lines that meet at the poles.

questions.question: 11. The distance between longitudes is maximum at? / देशांतरों के बीच की दूरी कहाँ अधिकतम होती है?
questions.explanation: Longitude lines are furthest apart at the Equator (111.3 km) and meet at the poles.

questions.question: 12. 0° Longitude is also known as? / 0° देशांतर को किस नाम से भी जाना जाता है?
options: Indian Standard Time / भारतीय मानक समय
International Date Line / अंतर्राष्ट्रीय तिथि रेखा
questions.explanation: The 0° longitude is the Prime Meridian, passing through Greenwich, UK.

questions.question: 13. How much time does Earth take to rotate 1° longitude? / पृथ्वी को 1° देशांतर घूमने में कितना समय लगता है?
questions.explanation: Since Earth rotates 360° in 24 hours, 1° of rotation takes 4 minutes.

questions.question: 14. The International Date Line (IDL) is situated at? / अंतर्राष्ट्रीय तिथि रेखा (IDL) कहाँ स्थित है?
questions.explanation: The 180° longitude is designated as the International Date Line.

questions.question: 15. Through which Strait does the International Date Line pass? / अंतर्राष्ट्रीय तिथि रेखा किस जलडमरूमध्य से होकर गुजरती है?
questions.explanation: The IDL passes through the Bering Strait in a zigzag manner to avoid land.

questions.question: 16. What is the longitude of Indian Standard Time (IST)? / भारतीय मानक समय (IST) का देशांतर क्या है?
questions.explanation: IST is based on the 82° 30' E longitude passing through Mirzapur/Prayagraj.

questions.question: 17. IST is how many hours ahead of GMT? / IST, GMT से कितने घंटे आगे है?
questions.explanation: Indian Standard Time is 5 hours and 30 minutes ahead of Greenwich Mean Time.

questions.question: 18. On which date is the Summer Solstice (longest day in Northern Hemisphere)? / ग्रीष्म संक्रांति (उत्तरी गोलार्ध में सबसे लंबा दिन) किस तिथि को होती है?
questions.explanation: On June 21, the Sun is directly overhead the Tropic of Cancer.

questions.question: 19. When do we have equal day and night (Equinox)? / समान दिन और रात (विषुव) कब होते हैं?
questions.explanation: Equinox occurs when the Sun is directly above the Equator, making day and night equal.

questions.question: 20. What causes the change of seasons? / ऋतु परिवर्तन का क्या कारण है?
options: Distance from Moon / चंद्रमा से दूरी
questions.explanation: Seasons change due to Earth's revolution around the Sun and its tilted axis (23.5°).

questions.question: 21. What is the region between the Tropic of Cancer and Tropic of Capricorn called? / कर्क और मकर रेखा के बीच के क्षेत्र को क्या कहा जाता है?
questions.explanation: The Torrid Zone receives maximum heat as the Sun is directly overhead at least once a year.

questions.question: 22. Spring Tides occur when? / वृहत ज्वार कब आते हैं?
options: During Eclipses only / केवल ग्रहण के दौरान
Sun, Earth, Moon are in line / सूर्य, पृथ्वी, चंद्रमा एक रेखा में हों
Earth is closest to Sun / पृथ्वी सूर्य के सबसे करीब हो
Sun and Moon are at 90° / सूर्य और चंद्रमा 90° पर हों
questions.explanation: Spring tides occur during Full Moon and New Moon when gravitational forces combine.

questions.question: 23. Neap Tides occur when? / निम्न ज्वार कब आते हैं?
options: Sun, Earth, Moon are in line / सूर्य, पृथ्वी, चंद्रमा एक रेखा में हों
During Summer Solstice / ग्रीष्म संक्रांति के दौरान
Sun and Moon are at 90° / सूर्य और चंद्रमा 90° पर हों
questions.explanation: Neap tides occur when the Sun and Moon’s gravitational pulls are perpendicular.

questions.question: 24. What is the time interval between two high tides? / दो उच्च ज्वार के बीच का समय अंतराल क्या है?
questions.explanation: Due to the Moon's revolution, high tides occur every 12 hours and 25 minutes.

questions.question: 25. The change from low tide to high tide is called? / निम्न ज्वार से उच्च ज्वार में परिवर्तन को क्या कहा जाता है?
questions.explanation: Rising water level is called 'Flood' or 'Flow' tide.

questions.question: 26. Which country has the maximum number of Time Zones? / किस देश में सबसे अधिक समय क्षेत्र हैं?
questions.explanation: France has 12 time zones (including overseas territories), the most of any country.

questions.question: 27. At what angle is the Earth's axis tilted from the vertical line? / पृथ्वी की धुरी लंबवत रेखा से कितने कोण पर झुकी हुई है?
questions.explanation: Earth's axis is tilted 23.5° from the vertical and 66.5° from the orbital plane.

questions.question: 28. "Perihelion" (closest distance to Sun) occurs on? / "उपसौर" (सूर्य से निकटतम दूरी) कब होती है?
questions.explanation: Perihelion occurs around Jan 3rd when Earth is approx 147 million km from the Sun.

questions.question: 29. "Aphelion" (farthest distance from Sun) occurs on? / "अपसौर" (सूर्य से अधिकतम दूरी) कब होती है?
questions.explanation: Aphelion occurs around July 4th when Earth is approx 152 million km from the Sun.

questions.question: 30. In which ocean is the International Date Line mostly located? / अंतर्राष्ट्रीय तिथि रेखा मुख्य रूप से किस महासागर में स्थित है?
questions.explanation: The IDL passes through the mid-Pacific Ocean following the 180° meridian.

questions.question: 1. Who gave the 'Big Bang Theory' in 1931? / 1931 में 'बिग बैंग थ्योरी' किसने दी थी?
questions.explanation: George Lemaitre proposed the Big Bang Theory in 1931 to explain the origin of the universe.

questions.question: 2. Which is the nearest galaxy to the Milky Way? / मिल्की वे की सबसे निकटतम आकाशगंगा कौन सी है?
questions.explanation: The Andromeda Galaxy is the closest major galaxy to our own Milky Way.

questions.question: 3. The study of the Universe is known as? / ब्रह्मांड के अध्ययन को क्या कहा जाता है?
questions.explanation: Cosmology is the scientific study of the origin and development of the universe.

questions.question: 4. Which theory explains the formation of the Sun from a giant cloud of dust and gas? / धूल और गैस के एक विशाल बादल से सूर्य के निर्माण की व्याख्या कौन सा सिद्धांत करता है?
options: Steady State Theory / स्टेडी स्टेट थ्योरी
Big Bang Theory / बिग बैंग थ्योरी
questions.explanation: Nebula Theory (1755) suggests the Sun formed from a giant cloud called a Nebula.

questions.question: 5. What is the main component of the Sun (approx 72-73%)? / सूर्य का मुख्य घटक (लगभग 72-73%) क्या है?
questions.explanation: The Sun is primarily composed of Hydrogen (72-73%) and Helium.

questions.question: 6. Which is the largest constellation in the sky? / आकाश में सबसे बड़ा नक्षत्र कौन सा है?
questions.explanation: Hydra is identified as the largest constellation among the groups of stars.

questions.question: 7. Which star is known as the "Dog Star" and is the brightest in the night sky? / किस तारे को "डॉग स्टार" कहा जाता है और यह रात के आकाश में सबसे चमकीला है?
questions.explanation: Sirius (Dog Star) is the brightest star visible from Earth in the night sky.

questions.question: 8. How much time does Sun's light take to reach Earth? / सूर्य के प्रकाश को पृथ्वी तक पहुँचने में कितना समय लगता है?
questions.explanation: It takes approximately 8 minutes and 30 seconds for sunlight to reach the Earth's surface.

questions.question: 9. Which planet is known as "Earth's Twin"? / किस ग्रह को "पृथ्वी का जुड़वां" कहा जाता है?
questions.explanation: Venus is called Earth's Twin because of its similar size and mass.

questions.question: 10. Which is the hottest planet in our Solar System? / हमारे सौर मंडल का सबसे गर्म ग्रह कौन सा है?
questions.explanation: Venus is the hottest planet due to thick clouds of CO2 and H2SO4 that trap heat.

questions.question: 11. Which planet is called the "Red Planet"? / किस ग्रह को "लाल ग्रह" कहा जाता है?
questions.explanation: Mars is known as the Red Planet due to the presence of iron oxide in its soil.

questions.question: 12. The Asteroid Belt lies between which two planets? / क्षुद्रग्रह पट्टी किन दो ग्रहों के बीच स्थित है?
options: Earth and Mars / पृथ्वी और मंगल
Mars and Jupiter / मंगल और बृहस्पति
Jupiter and Saturn / बृहस्पति और शनि
Venus and Earth / शुक्र और पृथ्वी
questions.explanation: The Asteroid Belt is a region located between the orbits of Mars and Jupiter.

questions.question: 13. Which is the largest moon (satellite) in our Solar System? / हमारे सौर मंडल का सबसे बड़ा चंद्रमा (उपग्रह) कौन सा है?
questions.explanation: Ganymede, a moon of Jupiter, is the largest satellite in the entire solar system.

questions.question: 14. Which planet has the maximum number of moons (146)? / किस ग्रह के चंद्रमाओं की संख्या सबसे अधिक (146) है?
questions.explanation: Saturn currently holds the record for the most moons, with a total of 146.

questions.question: 15. Which planet is known as the "Green Planet" due to Methane? / मीथेन के कारण किस ग्रह को "हरा ग्रह" कहा जाता है?
questions.explanation: Uranus appears greenish in color because of the methane gas in its atmosphere.

questions.question: 16. Which planet is tilted at 98° and is called the "Rolling Planet"? / कौन सा ग्रह 98° पर झुका हुआ है और इसे "लुढ़कता हुआ ग्रह" कहा जाता है?
questions.explanation: Uranus is tilted significantly on its axis, giving it a "rolling" appearance.

questions.question: 17. Which is the windiest planet in the Solar System? / सौर मंडल का सबसे हवादार ग्रह कौन सा है?
questions.explanation: Neptune is recognized as the windiest planet in our solar system.

questions.question: 18. In which year was Pluto reclassified as a "Dwarf Planet"? / प्लूटो को किस वर्ष "बौना ग्रह" के रूप में पुनर्वर्गीकृत किया गया था?
questions.explanation: The IAU reclassified Pluto as a dwarf planet in 2006.

questions.question: 19. Halley's Comet is visible every how many years? / हैली का धूमकेतु हर कितने साल में दिखाई देता है?
questions.explanation: Halley's Comet is a periodic comet that appears every 76 years.

questions.question: 20. Which is the smallest planet in our Solar System? / हमारे सौर मंडल का सबसे छोटा ग्रह कौन सा है?
questions.explanation: Mercury is the closest planet to the Sun and the smallest in the solar system.

questions.question: 21. Which planets have no natural satellites (moons)? / किन ग्रहों का कोई प्राकृतिक उपग्रह (चंद्रमा) नहीं है?
options: Mercury and Venus / बुध और शुक्र
Venus and Earth / शुक्र और पृथ्वी
Mercury and Mars / बुध और मंगल
Mars and Venus / मंगल और शुक्र
questions.explanation: Both Mercury and Venus are unique as they do not have any moons.

questions.question: 22. What is the name of India's first Solar Mission? / भारत के पहले सौर मिशन का नाम क्या है?
questions.explanation: Aditya L1 is ISRO's first dedicated mission to study the Sun.

questions.question: 23. Which planet has the shortest rotation period (approx 10 hours)? / किस ग्रह की घूर्णन अवधि सबसे कम (लगभग 10 घंटे) है?
questions.explanation: Jupiter has the fastest rotation of all planets, completing a turn in under 10 hours.

questions.question: 24. Which planet is known as the "Winter Planet"? / किस ग्रह को "शीतकालीन ग्रह" कहा जाता है?
questions.explanation: Jupiter is often referred to as the "Winter Planet" due to its low temperatures.

questions.question: 25. The gap between Saturn's rings is known as? / शनि के छल्लों के बीच के अंतर को क्या कहा जाता है?
questions.explanation: The Cassini Division is the famous gap located between Saturn's A and B rings.

questions.question: 26. Which dwarf planet is located in the Asteroid Belt? / क्षुद्रग्रह पट्टी में कौन सा बौना ग्रह स्थित है?
questions.explanation: Ceres is the only dwarf planet located within the inner solar system's asteroid belt.

questions.question: 27. What is the brightest star in the Orion constellation? / ओरियन नक्षत्र का सबसे चमकीला तारा कौन सा है?
questions.explanation: Rigel is the brightest star within the Orion constellation.

questions.question: 28. "Triton" is the famous moon of which planet? / "ट्राइटन" किस ग्रह का प्रसिद्ध चंद्रमा है?
questions.explanation: Triton is the largest and most famous natural satellite of Neptune.

questions.question: 29. Which planet rotates clockwise (East to West)? / कौन सा ग्रह दक्षिणावर्त (पूर्व से पश्चिम) घूमता है?
questions.explanation: Most planets rotate West to East, but Venus and Uranus rotate East to West.

questions.question: 30. What is a "Supernova"? / "सुपरनोवा" क्या है?
options: A black hole / एक ब्लैक होल
A neutron star / एक न्यूट्रॉन तारा
Explosion of a star / तारे का विस्फोट
A group of stars / तारों का एक समूह
questions.explanation: A supernova is a powerful and luminous stellar explosion.
